verses.text: Darius sets Daniel over the realm, 'preferred above the presidents' for his excellent spirit. Honoured by faith. God's servant rises by integrity, not intrigue.
Darius sets Daniel over the realm, 'preferred above the presidents' for his excellent spirit. Honoured by faith. God's servant rises by integrity, not intrigue.
Darius sets Daniel over the realm, 'preferred above the presidents' for his excellent spirit. Honoured by faith. God's servant rises by integrity, not intrigue.
His rivals seek a fault but find none, save 'concerning the law of his God.' Spotless. The only charge they could bring was his faithfulness to God.
His rivals seek a fault but find none, save 'concerning the law of his God.' Spotless. The only charge they could bring was his faithfulness to God.
They flatter the king into a decree that none may pray to any but him for thirty days, or face the lions. A trap baited. Pride is easily snared by those who feed it.
They flatter the king into a decree that none may pray to any but him for thirty days, or face the lions. A trap baited. Pride is easily snared by those who feed it.
They flatter the king into a decree that none may pray to any but him for thirty days, or face the lions. A trap baited. Pride is easily snared by those who feed it.
They flatter the king into a decree that none may pray to any but him for thirty days, or face the lions. A trap baited. Pride is easily snared by those who feed it.
Daniel, knowing the decree, goes home and prays toward Jerusalem three times a day 'as he did aforetime.' Steadfast prayer. He will not hide his devotion to save his life.
Daniel, knowing the decree, goes home and prays toward Jerusalem three times a day 'as he did aforetime.' Steadfast prayer. He will not hide his devotion to save his life.
They report him to the king, who is displeased to find himself caught. Conscience too late. The flatterers' trap closes on the one they flattered.
They report him to the king, who is displeased to find himself caught. Conscience too late. The flatterers' trap closes on the one they flattered.
The king labours to save Daniel, but the law cannot be changed, and they press him. Power helpless. The proud decree binds even the king who made it.
The king labours to save Daniel, but the law cannot be changed, and they press him. Power helpless. The proud decree binds even the king who made it.
Daniel is cast into the den; the king says, 'Thy God... will deliver thee,' seals the stone, and fasts sleepless. Anxious hope. Even a heathen king trusts the God of His faithful servant.
Daniel is cast into the den; the king says, 'Thy God... will deliver thee,' seals the stone, and fasts sleepless. Anxious hope. Even a heathen king trusts the God of His faithful servant.
Daniel is cast into the den; the king says, 'Thy God... will deliver thee,' seals the stone, and fasts sleepless. Anxious hope. Even a heathen king trusts the God of His faithful servant.
At dawn the king cries, 'O Daniel... is thy God... able to deliver thee from the lions?' Eager longing. The night of dread ends in a hopeful question.
At dawn the king cries, 'O Daniel... is thy God... able to deliver thee from the lions?' Eager longing. The night of dread ends in a hopeful question.
Daniel answers, 'My God hath sent his angel, and hath shut the lions' mouths,' for innocency was found in him; he is taken up unhurt. Faith vindicated. God guards the one who trusts Him fully.
Daniel answers, 'My God hath sent his angel, and hath shut the lions' mouths,' for innocency was found in him; he is taken up unhurt. Faith vindicated. God guards the one who trusts Him fully.
Daniel answers, 'My God hath sent his angel, and hath shut the lions' mouths,' for innocency was found in him; he is taken up unhurt. Faith vindicated. God guards the one who trusts Him fully.
The accusers are cast in and devoured, and Darius proclaims peace to all peoples. Justice turns. The pit they dug becomes their own grave.
The accusers are cast in and devoured, and Darius proclaims peace to all peoples. Justice turns. The pit they dug becomes their own grave.
The king decrees that all tremble before 'the living God,' whose kingdom endures; and Daniel prospers. A wide witness. One man's faith brought a nation to honour God.
